perturbation.original_text: For a period of eighteen months following the execution of this agreement to the Receiving Party shall not, directly or indirectly hire, solicit, or encourage to leave the Disclosing Party employm ent, any employee, contractor or player of the Disclosing Party or hire any such employee or contractor who has left the Disclosing Parties employment, contractual engagement or player within one year of such employment or engagement , the Receiving Party .
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of eighteen months following the execution of this agreement, the Receiving Party may, directly or indirectly, hire, solicit, or encourage to leave the Disclosing Party employment, any employee, contractor or player of the Disclosing Party or hire any such employee or contractor who has left the Disclosing Parties employment, contractual engagement or player within one year of such employment or engagement, the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not' to 'may', this introduces a contradiction. The original text explicitly prohibits the Receiving Party from hiring/soliciting employees from the Disclosing Party. The modified text suggests the Receiving Party is allowed to do so. This contradicts the general non-solicitation intent in the original agreement, creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the disclosure of Proprietary Information by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party hereby agrees: (i) to hold the Proprietary Information in str ict confidence and to take all reasonable precautions to protect such Proprietary Information (including, without limitation, all precautions the Receiving Party employs with respect to its own confidential materials), (ii) not to disclose any such Proprie tary Information or any information derived there from to any third person, (iii) not to make any use whatsoever at any time of such Proprietary Information except to evaluate internally its relationship with the Disclosing Par ty, and (iv) not to copy or r everse engineer any such Proprietary Information. The Receiving Party shall procure that its employees, agents and sub -contractors to whom Proprietary Information is disclosed or who have access to Proprietary Information sign a nondisclosure or similar ag reement in content substantially similar to this Agreement . For a period of eighteen months following the execution of this agreement, the Receiving Party may, directly or indirectly, hire, solicit, or encourage to leave the Disclosing Party employment, any employee, contractor or player of the Disclosing Party or hire any such employee or contractor who has left the Disclosing Parties employment, contractual engagement or player within one year of such employment or engagement, the Receiving Party . For a period of three years following the execution of this agreement , the contractor shall not start up his/her own cricket related business using the customer lists, trade secrets, inventions, innovations, processes, information, records an d specificatio ns of the Disclosing Party and/or used by the Disclosing party in connection with the operation of its business including, without limitation, the Disclosing parties business and product processes, methods, accounts and procedures.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'shall not' to 'may', which creates a direct contradiction. The original text forbids the Receiving Party from soliciting employees, but the changed text permits it. The contradicted text, which includes the changed text itself, initially suggests an agreement to hold Proprietary Information in strict confidence, implying a non-solicitation agreement. However, it then contradicts itself by stating the Receiving Party 'may' solicit employees. This creates a structural flaw and introduces uncertainty, fitting the definition of a Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of three years following the execution of this agreement , the contractor shall not start up his/her own cricket related business using the customer lists, trade secrets, inventions, innovations, processes, information, records an d specificatio ns of the Disclosing Party and/or used by the Disclosing party in connection with the operation of its business including, without limitation, the Disclosing parties business and product processes, methods, accounts and procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of three years following the execution of this agreement, the contractor is encouraged to start up his/her own cricket related business using the customer lists, trade secrets, inventions, innovations, processes, information, records and specifications of the Disclosing Party and/or used by the Disclosing party in connection with the operation of its business including, without limitation, the Disclosing parties business and product processes, methods, accounts and procedures.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not' to 'is encouraged to', this introduces a direct contradiction. The original text prevents the contractor from starting a competing business using the Disclosing Party's assets. The changed text now explicitly encourages the contractor to do so. This is a contradiction of the NDA's intent to protect Proprietary Information and creates ambiguity about permissible actions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the disclosure of Proprietary Information by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party hereby agrees: (i) to hold the Proprietary Information in str ict confidence and to take all reasonable precautions to protect such Proprietary Information (including, without limitation, all precautions the Receiving Party employs with respect to its own confidential materials), (ii) not to disclose any such Proprie tary Information or any information derived there from to any third person, (iii) not to make any use whatsoever at any time of such Proprietary Information except to evaluate internally its relationship with the Disclosing Par ty, and (iv) not to copy or r everse engineer any such Proprietary Information. The Receiving Party shall procure that its employees, agents and sub -contractors to whom Proprietary Information is disclosed or who have access to Proprietary Information sign a nondisclosure or similar ag reement in content substantially similar to this Agreement . For a period of eighteen months following the execution of this agreement, the Receiving Party may, directly or indirectly, hire, solicit, or encourage to leave the Disclosing Party employment, any employee, contractor or player of the Disclosing Party or hire any such employee or contractor who has left the Disclosing Parties employment, contractual engagement or player within one year of such employment or engagement, the Receiving Party. For a period of three years following the execution of this agreement, the contractor is encouraged to start up his/her own cricket related business using the customer lists, trade secrets, inventions, innovations, processes, information, records and specifications of the Disclosing Party and/or used by the Disclosing party in connection with the operation of its business including, without limitation, the Disclosing parties business and product processes, methods, accounts and procedures.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibited the contractor from starting a competing business using the Disclosing Party's assets. The changed text encourages the contractor to do exactly that. This creates a direct conflict with the 'Contradicted Text' which states that the Receiving Party agrees to hold the Proprietary Information in strict confidence and not to make any use whatsoever of such Proprietary Information. Encouraging the contractor to use the Disclosing Party's assets directly contradicts this requirement of strict confidence and no use, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction, according to the definition 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Without granting any rig ht or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any informa tion after five years following the disclosure thereof or any information that the Receiving Party can document (i) is or becomes (through no improper action or inaction by the Receiving Party or any affiliate, agent, consultant or employee) generally available to the public, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party as evidenced in writing, exce pt to the extent that such information was unlawfully appropriated, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Without granting any rig ht or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall apply with respect to any informa tion after five years following the disclosure thereof or any information that the Receiving Party can document (i) is or becomes (through no improper action or inaction by the Receiving Party or any affiliate, agent, consultant or employee) generally available to the public, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party as evidenced in writing, exce pt to the extent that such information was unlawfully appropriated, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not apply' to 'shall apply' directly contradicts the intent of the exception clause. Initially, information becoming publicly available after five years was *not* subject to the NDA. Now, it *is* subject to the NDA for an indefinite period after five years from disclosure if it fits any of the (i) through (iv) conditions. This introduces substantial uncertainty regarding the term of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without granting any rig ht or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall apply with respect to any informa tion after five years following the disclosure thereof or any information that the Receiving Party can document (i) is or becomes (through no improper action or inaction by the Receiving Party or any affiliate, agent, consultant or employee) generally available to the public, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party as evidenced in writing, exce pt to the extent that such information was unlawfully appropriated, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party may make disclosure s required by law or court order provided the Receiving Party uses diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure and has allowed the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'shall not apply' to 'shall apply'. According to the perturbation explanation, this inverts the original clause's meaning. Initially, information becoming publicly available after five years was not subject to the NDA. Now, it is. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text and highlights that now that it applies, it creates substantial uncertainty regarding the term of confidentiality. Therefore, the change creates a direct contradiction, thus it is a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: Immediately upon the written request by the Disclosing Party at any time, th e Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copies or extr acts thereof, save that where such Proprietary Information is a form inca pable of return or has been copied or transcribed into another document, it shall be destroyed or erased, as appropriate.
perturbation.changed_text: Immediately upon the written request by the Disclosing Party at any time, th e Receiving Party will retain all Proprietary Information and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copies or extr acts thereof, save that where such Proprietary Information is a form inca pable of return or has been copied or transcribed into another document, it shall be destroyed or erased, as appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'return' to 'retain' creates a clear contradiction. The original clause mandates the return of proprietary information upon request. The changed version now instructs the Receiving Party to keep the information. This fundamentally undermines the purpose of the NDA, which is to protect the Disclosing Party's confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Immediately upon the written request by the Disclosing Party at any time, th e Receiving Party will retain all Proprietary Information and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copies or extr acts thereof, save that where such Proprietary Information is a form inca pable of return or has been copied or transcribed into another document, it shall be destroyed or erased, as appropriate.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Receiving Party will return Proprietary Information, while the changed text states that the Receiving Party will retain all Proprietary Information. This creates a direct conflict because 'return' and 'retain' are antonyms. The Contradicted Text is in line with the Changed Text, however, since the question asks whether the Change causes a contradiction with the Contradicted Text, and the Change clearly does based on the description, the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party may make disclosure s required by law or court order provided the Receiving Party uses diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure and has allowed the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may make disclosure s required by law or court order without any obligation to limit disclosure or allowing the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement to limit disclosure and allow the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order, the text now suggests the Receiving Party can freely disclose Proprietary Information if compelled by law, without any effort to mitigate the harm to the Disclosing Party. This directly contradicts the spirit of confidentiality and puts the Disclosing Party at risk. It also potentially negates previous sections requiring confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without granting any rig ht or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall apply with respect to any informa tion after five years following the disclosure thereof or any information that the Receiving Party can document (i) is or becomes (through no improper action or inaction by the Receiving Party or any affiliate, agent, consultant or employee) generally available to the public, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party as evidenced in writing, exce pt to the extent that such information was unlawfully appropriated, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party may make disclosure s required by law or court order without any obligation to limit disclosure or allowing the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for the Receiving Party to limit disclosure or allow the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order when disclosures are required by law. This directly contradicts the principle of confidentiality implied in the contradicted text, specifically regarding exceptions to confidentiality obligations, and creates a scenario where the Receiving Party can freely disclose information without mitigation, which is against the spirit of the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: The failure of either party to enforce its rights under this Agreement at any time for any period shall not be construed as a waiver of such rights.
perturbation.changed_text: The failure of either party to enforce its rights under this Agreement at any time for any period shall be construed as a waiver of such rights.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not be construed' to 'shall be construed' reverses the meaning of the waiver clause. Originally, inaction was *not* a waiver. Now, it *is* a waiver. This creates a contradiction: rights are established in earlier sections, but this clause makes it easy to lose those rights through inaction, leading to enforcement uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The failure of either party to enforce its rights under this Agreement at any time for any period shall be construed as a waiver of such rights. If any part, term or provision of this Agr eement is held to be illegal or unenforceable neither the validity, nor enforceability of the remainder of this Agreement shall be affected. Neither Party shall assign or transfer all or any part o f its rights under this Agreement without the consent of th e other Party. This Agreement may not be amended for any other reason without the prior written agreement of both Parties. This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof unless any representati on or warranty made about this Agreement was made fraudulently and, save as may be expressly referred to or referenced herein, supersedes all prior representations, writings, negotiations or understandings with respect hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that failure to enforce rights shall NOT be construed as a waiver. The changed text reverses this, stating that it SHALL be construed as a waiver. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, thereby creating a direct conflict because now inaction leads to loss of rights, which the original contract structure aims to protect, causing enforcement uncertainty, therefore it's a contradiction.
